channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: NTSB says United jet was too slow and too low in Newark landing accident
item.description: Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.
item.content:encoded:
Federal investigators say the captain flying the United 767 from Italy was too slow and too low before landing last month at Newark, N.J. The jet struck a light pole, damaging a truck on the turnpike.

(Image credit: Joshua A. Bickel)
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis

item.title: New York City reshapes mass transit system to handle World Cup, NBA finals crowds
item.description: New York transit officials are preparing to handle up to 100,000 extra travelers a day as fans arrive in New York and New Jersey for FIFA World Cup matches.
item.content:encoded:
New York transit officials are preparing to handle up to 100,000 extra travelers a day as fans arrive in New York and New Jersey for FIFA World Cup matches.

(Image credit: ANGELA WEISS/AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Embedded: "We Keep Us Safe" from NPR, KUOW and The Seattle Times
item.description: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.
item.content:encoded: In the summer of 2020, sixteen-year-old Antonio Mays Jr. traveled a thousand miles to join the racial justice movement of his generation. He arrived in Seattle during the Capitol Hill Occupied Protest, known as CHOP. Less than a week later, he was shot and killed there. The case remains unsolved.

item.title: Gun control group sues ATF over records release
item.description: Brady, a nonprofit gun control advocacy group, is suing the ATF and the DOJ over their refusals to release documents and other information about who the largest sellers of crime guns in the U.S. are.
item.content:encoded:
Brady, a nonprofit gun control advocacy group, is suing the ATF and the DOJ over their refusals to release documents and other information about who the largest sellers of crime guns in the U.S. are.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Jaclyn Diaz

item.title: How cellphone carriers prepare for hurricane season with AI, drones and 'cows'
item.description: Hurricane season is expected to be milder than usual this year. But that's not stopping cell phone companies from pulling out all the stops.
item.content:encoded:
Hurricane season is expected to be milder than usual this year. But that's not stopping cell phone companies from pulling out all the stops.

(Image credit: Melissa Sue Gerrits)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: The $13 billion carrier with a plumbing problem is home. Now come the costly repairs
item.description: With 4,600 sailors finally home, USS Gerald R. Ford will finally receive some much needed repairs and an upgrade to its beleaguered sewage system.
item.content:encoded:
With 4,600 sailors finally home, USS Gerald R. Ford will finally receive some much needed repairs and an upgrade to its beleaguered sewage system.

(Image credit: Mike Kropf)
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: National Guard has done little to reduce violent crime in D.C., a new study finds
item.description: A new study has found that the National Guard's presence in Washington, D.C. had no effect on violent crime in the city. The Guard has been deployed since last August as part of a federal task force to fight crime, and their numbers are set to double in the coming weeks.
item.content:encoded:
A new study has found that the National Guard's presence in Washington, D.C. had no effect on violent crime in the city. The Guard has been deployed since last August as part of a federal task force to fight crime, and their numbers are set to double in the coming weeks.

(Image credit: Alex Wroblewski)
item.dc:creator: Kat Lonsdorf

item.title: Screens are leaving schools fast, though some students with disabilities rely on them
item.description: Some students with disabilities rely on assistive technology to learn, and they worry it could be swept up in the movement to get screens out of schools.
item.content:encoded:
Some students with disabilities rely on assistive technology to learn, and they worry it could be swept up in the movement to get screens out of schools.

(Image credit: Jonaki Mehta)
item.dc:creator: Jonaki Mehta

item.title: The Obama Presidential Center will open soon. Here's a sneak peek
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center opens later this month. NPR got a preview.
item.content:encoded: The Obama Presidential Center opens later this month. NPR got a preview.
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Critics say adding Trump's arch to D.C. would take something away: the view
item.description: A second federal agency advanced Trump's arch proposal on Thursday, but requested more information about its implications for everything from the landscape to pedestrian safety to aviation.
item.content:encoded: A second federal agency advanced Trump's arch proposal on Thursday, but requested more information about its implications for everything from the landscape to pedestrian safety to aviation.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman
